sales.title:
EMPIRE ROMAIN - ROMAN
Vespasien (69-79). Aureus ND (69-71), Rome. NGC Ch XF 5/5 4/5 (6639766-001).
Av. IMP CAESAR VESPASIANVS AVG. Tête laurée à droite.
Rv. COS ITER - TR POT. L’Équité à gauche avec un sceptre et les balances.
Calicó 604 - RIC.5 ; Or - 7,23 g - 19 mm - 5 h
NGC Ch XF 5/5 4/5 (6639766-001).
Les champs sont brunis mais le brillant d’origine reste visible autour des reliefs. Faible usure. Superbe.

Vespasian (AD 69-79). AV aureus (19mm, 7.30 gm, 7h). NGC MS 5/5 - 4/5. Rome, January-June AD 70. IMP CAESAR VESPASIANVS AVG, laureate head of Vespasian right / COS ITER-T-R POT, Pax seated le
sales.description:
Ancients
Vespasian (AD 69-79). AV aureus (19mm, 7.30 gm, 7h). NGC MS 5/5 - 4/5. Rome, January-June AD 70. IMP CAESAR VESPASIANVS AVG, laureate head of Vespasian right / COS ITER-T-R POT, Pax seated left, branch in raised right hand, caduceus cradled in left arm. Calicó 607. RIC II.1, 28. This exceptional specimen features captivating auburn toning delicately dispersed across its surface, a characteristic frequently associated with the renowned Boscoreale Hoard.

Ex Heritage Auctions, Auction 3...

sales.description:
Ancients
Vespasian (AD 69-79). AV aureus (18mm, 7.26 gm, 7h). NGC Choice XF 5/5 - 3/5, brushed. Lugdunum, AD 69-71. IMP CAESAR VESPASIANVS AVG TR P, laureate head of Vespasian right / COS ITER-FORT RED, Fortuna standing facing, head left, with right hand on prow left, cornucopia cradled in left arm. Calicó 602a. RIC II.1, 1104. Crisp, well-centered dies. 

Descended from a family of small-time entrepreneurs and tax farmers in the Sabine hill country north of Rome, Flavius Vespasianus was born ...

sales.description:
Ancients
Domitian, as Caesar (AD 81-96). AV aureus (20mm, 7.34 gm, 6h). NGC AU 5/5 - 4/5, Fine Style, brushed. Rome, AD 76-77. CAESAR AVG F-DOMITIANVS, laureate head of Domitian right / COS-IIII, cornucopia bound with fillet, containing grain ears, pyramidal cake, pomegranates and other fruits, three grape clusters hanging from rim of horn. Calicó 817. RIC II.1 (Vespasian) 918. A bold portrait captured on radiating dies.

Ex McLendon Collection (Christie's, 12 June 1993), lot 111; Leu Numismat...

sales.title: Ira & Larry Goldberg Coins & Collectibles - Auction 150, Lot 591 — Vespasian, AD 69-79. Gold Aureus (7.2g) 20 mm. Mint of Rome AD 69/70. Laur. bust of the emperor facing r. Reverse: Titus and Domitian standing holding patera and roll. Calicõ 592 R-2. NGC graded VF*;
sales.description:
Vespasian, AD 69-79. Gold Aureus (7.2g) 20 mm. Mint of Rome AD 69/70. Laur. bust of the emperor facing r. Reverse: Titus and Domitian standing holding patera and roll. Calicõ 592 R-2. NGC graded VF*; Strike: 5/5, Surface: 5/5. A Premium Quality example thus the Star designation. Boldly struck with full legends on both sides. Deep rose color toning within the devices. Rare.  Estimated Value $3,000 - UP
Ex The Peter Preovolos Collection; Ex NAC O (5/2004); 1934 The Morris Collection....
